document.title: EN 62220-1-1 Test Report for A1717MCW / A1417MCW / F1417MCW Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

sourceSummary.oneLine: EN 62220-1-1 test report for A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW digital flat panel X-ray detectors.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports technical documentation by reporting detector physical, operational, functional, exposure, safety, and test-result characteristics.
keyPoints: The report states that A1717MCW, A1417MCW and F1417MCW performance and usage are the same except for size.
The applicable standard is EN 62220-1-1 Edition 1.0 2015-03 for DQE determination of detectors used in radiographic imaging.
The detector uses a CsI scintillator and converts x-ray energy to visible light, then to electrical signals in photodiode arrays.
The device is not for fluoroscopic use and is fixed at 1 fps because it is a static x-ray detector.
Reported test results include DQE, MTF, NPS, linearity and sensitivity, lag, defect criteria, and flat field correction data.

concepts.name: EN 62220-1-1
concepts.evidence: Test report was provided for Solid-State X-ray Imaging Devices, which includes MTF and DQE measurements as tested by EN 62220-1-1(2015)

concepts.name: Detective Quantum Efficiency
concepts.evidence: The detective quantum efficiency (DQE) was evaluated as a function of spatial frequency over a wide range of incident exposure levels.

concepts.name: Modulation Transfer Function
concepts.evidence: Provide a plot of the modulation or contrast transfer function of the device.

concepts.name: CsI scintillator
concepts.evidence: The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW device uses a CsI screen on a substrate.

concepts.name: Static x-ray detector
concepts.evidence: This device is fixed at 1 fps because it is a static x-ray detector.

concepts.name: Flat Field Correction
concepts.evidence: Performing Image processing Algorithm - Dark level Cancellation, Defect Correction, Flat Field Correction

concepts.name: DICOM
concepts.evidence: The saved raw data from A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW will be transferred to the end user’s S/W and then converted to a DICOM or Bitmap file.

concepts.name: 21CFR1020.31 or .32
concepts.evidence: Describe how x-ray beam alignment and collimation to the “active” area of the device will be achieved to meet the requirements of 21CFR1020.31 or .32.

evidence.claim: The report covers A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW digital flat panel X-ray detectors.
evidence.evidenceText: EN 62220-1-1 Test Report for A1717MCW / A1417MCW / F1417MCW Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: The report was provided for solid-state X-ray imaging devices and includes MTF and DQE measurements tested by EN 62220-1-1(2015).
evidence.evidenceText: Test report was provided for Solid-State X-ray Imaging Devices, which includes MTF and DQE measurements as tested by EN 62220-1-1(2015)

evidence.claim: Performance and usage of A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW are stated to be the same except for size.
evidence.evidenceText: Performance and usage are all the same expect for size. (A-TCF-5-127)

evidence.claim: The standard listed is EN 62220-1-1 Edition 1.0 2015-03 for DQE determination of detectors used in radiographic imaging.
evidence.evidenceText: 12-289 EN 62220-1-1 Edition 1.0 2015-03 Medical electrical equipment-Characteristics of digital X-ray imaging devices Part 1-1: Determination of the detective quantum efficiency Detectors used in radiographic imaging.

evidence.claim: A1717MCW entire dimensions including outer short ring are 460 mm by 460 mm and panel size is 430 mm by 430 mm.
evidence.evidenceText: A1717MCW ... Entire dimensions include outer short ring 460 (X) mm x 460 (Y) mm ... Panel size (outer short ring removed) 430 mm x 430 mm

evidence.claim: A1417MCW and F1417MCW entire dimensions including outer short ring are 384 mm by 460 mm and panel size is 358.4 mm by 430 mm.
evidence.evidenceText: A1417MCW ... Entire dimensions include outer short ring 384 (X) mm x 460 (Y) mm ... Panel size (outer short ring removed) 358.4 mm x 430 mm ... F1417MCW ... Entire dimensions include outer short ring 384 (X) mm x 460 (Y) mm ... Panel size (outer short ring removed) 358.4 mm x 430 mm

evidence.claim: The detector uses CsI as the scintillator and has a frame rate of 1 fps.
evidence.evidenceText: Scintillator • CsI ... Frame Rate • 1 fps

evidence.claim: The device generates a digital x-ray image in response to a trigger signal and does not initiate an X-ray generator or control patient exposure settings.
evidence.evidenceText: A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW generates a digital x-ray image in response to a trigger signal, but does not initiate an X-ray generator or control the x-ray exposure setting for a patient.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Operation

evidence.claim: The detector outputs raw image data via 1Gbps Ethernet or wireless to an access point.
evidence.evidenceText: The detector outputs raw image data to the workstation, via 1Gbps Ethernet or Wireless to Access Point.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Data Output

evidence.claim: The acquisition software receives 16-bit digital image data and stores it in 16-bit format.
evidence.evidenceText: The acquisition software in the workstation receives the 16-bit digital image data and processes it. It is stored in the hard disk in a 16-bit format.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Data Output

evidence.claim: The device is not for fluoroscopic use.
evidence.evidenceText: This device is not for fluoroscopic.

evidence.claim: A1717MCW DQE measured values are 79% at 0.1 lp/mm, 69% at 0.5 lp/mm, 62% at 1.0 lp/mm, 49% at 2.0 lp/mm, and 31% at 3.0 lp/mm.
A1417MCW DQE measured values are 78% at 0.1 lp/mm, 69% at 0.5 lp/mm, 61% at 1.0 lp/mm, 50% at 2.0 lp/mm, and 34% at 3.0 lp/mm.
F1417MCW DQE measured values are 76% at 0.1 lp/mm, 71% at 0.5 lp/mm, 64% at 1.0 lp/mm, 51% at 2.0 lp/mm, and 35% at 3.0 lp/mm.
MTF results are A1717MCW 84%, 65%, 30%, 13%; A1417MCW 85%, 65%, 33%, 17%; and F1417MCW 86%, 66%, 34%, 17% at listed spatial frequencies.
Aliasing artifacts are stated to be negligible because the pixel pitch is 0.14 mm and the sampling cut-off frequency is 3.57 lp/mm.
evidence.evidenceText: Its pitch of this system is 0.14 mm corresponding to a sampling cut-off frequency of 3.57 lp/mm. The MTF value at this frequency is approximately close to zero. ... artifacts in the image due to aliasing will be negligible.

evidence.claim: The device does not provide under-scanning functionality.
evidence.evidenceText: This device does not provide the applicable functionality.

evidence.claim: The minimum time between exposures is 8 seconds.
evidence.evidenceText: Readout of the detector after an x-ray exposure requires least 1 second with window 0.5 second. And to stabilize offset 7 seconds are needed. Thus, the minimum time between exposures is 8 seconds.

evidence.claim: The report states the devices meet regulatory standards outlined in safety report REP001824 and EMC reports NK-22-E-0811 and NK-22-E-0814.
evidence.evidenceText: The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW meets the regulatory standards as outlined in the A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW safety report: REP001824, EMC report: NK-22-E-0811, NK-22-E-0814

evidence.claim: Line chart exposure condition was 75 kVp, 100 mA, 10 mAs, SID 100 cm.
evidence.evidenceText: Exposure Condition - 75kVp, 100mA, 10mAs, SID: 100cm

evidence.claim: Reported linearity and sensitivity are A1717MCW 127.10 LSB/μGy, A1417MCW 126.74 LSB/uGy, and F1417MCW 121.5 LSB/uGy.
evidence.evidenceText: A1717MCW: 127.10 LSB/μGy ... A1417MCW: 126.74 LSB/uGy ... F1417MCW: 121.5 LSB/uGy
evidence.pageTarget: Digital X-ray Detector Linearity and Sensitivity

reviewFlags.reason: Authority is ambiguous: source metadata authoritySignal says MFDS/domestic, but the source text does not identify MFDS or a domestic regulatory authority.
The source path is under CE MDR documentation, but the source text does not state CE MDR, notified body, certificate status, or approval status.
Several formulas and some table entries appear degraded by extraction/OCR, including DQE formulas and entries such as '3.0 lp/mm (0 ≥ 31%)'.
F1417MCW quantum-limited performance section heading appears, but the extracted text does not provide dark noise or quantum noise limit values for F1417MCW.
The document lists regulatory standards via safety and EMC report references, but does not name the exact regulatory standards in section 5.1.
